title: Wear-by-pace interaction
summary: Combine tyre-life pressure with lap-time and degradation loss to identify tyres approaching a performance cliff.
rationale: Single-source tyre-age signals miss cases where a young tyre is performing poorly or an old tyre is still competitive. The run often combined tyre, degradation, and lap-time features.
implementation_hint: Create products or ratios between compound-scaled tyre age and positive lap-time delta, cumulative degradation, degradation per tyre life, and current-tyre finish pressure. Keep interactions limited to a compact set of physically meaningful pairs.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Interaction bloat was not consistently beneficial; use a small curated set and avoid exhaustive cross-products.
